REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MARIA GUERRERO, AMALIA GUERRERO, JOSE GUERRERO NOTICE OF MOTION FOR ORDER SETTING ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND ENTRY OF DEFAULT IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS ARE NOT ENTITLED TO LEGAL RELIEF UNDER CCP 473(B) AND DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SUSTAINED THEIR BURDEN THAT THEY ARE ENTITLED TO EQUITABLE RELIEF. ORDER FOR THE SAME SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Order Setting Aside Default Judgment And Entry Of Default
Off Calendar May-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Weiss, Esq. of City and County of San Francisco, 415-554-3824, appearing for City and County and San Francisco, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Marcus Dong, Esq. of Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck, 415-926-7634, appearing for Maria Guerrero, for Defendant and moving party.

Court reporter: Ken Brill. CSR #12797 telephone: (415)274-9977. email: sfdepo@veritext.com

Argued (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). Mr. Dong stated that he contested to the tentative ruling and requested that the default be set aside. Mr. Weiss provided case background and reported that the defendants are in default.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARIA GUERRERO, AMALIA GUERRERO, JOSE GUERRERO Notice Of Motion For Order Setting Aside Default Judgment And Entry Of Default is DENIED. Defendants are not entitled to legal relief under CCP 473(b) and Defendants have not sustained their burden that they are entitled to equitable relief. Order for the same signed in open court. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion For Order Setting Aside Default Judgment And Entry Of Default
Off Calendar May-02-2019 Continued To May-16-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Marcus M. Dong, Esq., 415-526-7634, for all Defendants.

Counsel appearing did not notify the other party about contesting the tentative ruling. Counsel requests hearing to be continued. Court reminds counsel about the standing rule in notifying parties re: contested tentative rulings. Court grants counsel's request for continuance.

DEFENDANTs MARIA GUERRERO, AMALIA GUERRERO and JOSE GUERRERO's Motion For Order Setting Aside Default Judgment And Entry Of Default is CONTINUED to MAY-16-2019 at 9:30am in Department 501.

MR. DONG IS ORDERED BY COURT TO GIVE NOTICE TO THE OTHER PARTY AND FOLLOW THE STANDING RULE ABOUT NOTIFYING PARTIES WHEN TENTATIVE RULING IS BEING CONTESTED.

Judge Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Maria Olopernes; Not Reported; D501.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-19-2018 Off Calendar

Motion For Default Judgment And Permanent Injunction Against Defendants Maria T. Guerrero, Amalia Guerrero, And Jose Guerrero
Off Calendar Sep-27-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Part 1 of 2
This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing. Dept. 514, Judge: Eric R. Fleming, Clerk: A. Mok. Proceedings reported by: Gina M. Kessler, CSR#8136.

Michael S. Weiss, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, City and County of San Francisco,
Order Granting Waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Maria T. Guerrero, Jose Guerrero, and Amalia Guerrero.
Default of Defendants has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendants as follows:

IT IS HEREBY ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED as follows:
DEFENDANTS are restrained and enjoined through an agent, officer, manager, representative, employee, or anyone else acting on DEFENDANTS' behalf, from doing any of the following:
a. maintaining the Property in condition as to constitute a public nuisance as defined by Civil Code sections 3479 and 3480 and the San Francisco Housing, Building, Plumbing, Electrical, Fire and Health Codes;
b. maintaining the Property in a condition as to violate the State Housing Law, codified as California Health and Safety Code sections 17910-17998.3;
c. maintaining the Property in such a condition as to violate the Unfair Competition Law, codified as Business and Professions Code sections 17200-17210,

DEFENDANTS are further ordered to obtain all required permits, cure all violations, and officially abate SF Department of Building Inspection by close of business on 10/31/2018; obtain all required permits; cure all violations, and officially abate DBI by close of business on 10/31/2018. Deliver copies each signed off, completed permit and all other proof of Compliance to the Office of the City Attorney, Attn: DCA Michael Weiss, Fox Plaza 1390 Market Street, 7th Floor, San Francisco, California 94102 by close of business on 12/31/2018.

In order to monitor compliance with this Injunction, DEFENDANTS shall allow, and PLAINTIFFS may conduct, periodic inspections of the interior and exterior of the Property. Refusal to permit inspection without good cause is a violation of this Order.

If any new violations of the SF Municipal Codes are discovered at the Property during the pendency of this Injunction, DEFENDANTS shall furnish PLAINTIFFS with a copy of any Notice of Violation, citation or administrative notice or order within 5 days of its issuance. DEFENDANTS shall abate the cited violations within the time period stated in any Notice of Violation, citation, or other administrative notice or order issued. Failure to timely comply is a violation of this Order.

If DEFENDANTS violate this Order, PLAINTIFFS may seek all remedies, relief and/or penalties including, the appointment of a receiver.

A violation of this Order may constitute contempt of Court and may be enforced through a contempt proceeding, a motion to enforce, or any other proceeding recognized by the Court for enforcement of an Injunction or Judgment.

In the event that the Court determines after hearing that DEFENDANTS violated any of the terms of this Order, DEFENDANTS shall be liable for civil penalties of up to $6,000 for each
violation, pursuant to Business and Professions Code section 17207.
(See next entry for mini minutes)

Motion For Default Judgment And Permanent Injunction Against Defendants Maria T. Guerrero, Amalia Guerrero, And Jose Guerrero (Duplicate Entry For Mini Minutes Only)
Off Calendar Sep-27-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Part 2 or 2
Mini minutes continued from previous entry

PLAINTIFFS shall be entitled to recover all reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred in monitoring and enforcing this Order. Any fines, penalties, or other relief specified herein for violating or failing to comply with this Order shall be in addition to any other relief or sanction that the Court may order as a matter of law or equity.

The terms of this Order shall expire eighty-four (84) months from the date of entry ("Injunctive Term"), if Defendants have complied and remained.in compliance throughout the Injunctive Term. If this Court finds that Defendants failed to comply with any of the terms of this Order during the Injunctive Term, then, in addition to any other relief available at law or equity, the Injunctive Term may be extended by the Court at the request of the Plaintiffs, or on the Court's own motion.

JUDGMENT
IT IS Further ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that PLAINTIFFS have Judgment against Defendants MARIA T. GUERRERO, AMALIA GUERRERO, and JOSE GUERRERO, jointly and severally as follows:
San Francisco Building Code Civil penalties $374,500
San Francisco Electrical Code civil penalties $507
Business and Professions Code civil penalties $147,500
Attorneys' fees $39,750.00
Costs $193.30.
Total $562,430.30 due by 4/1/2019
Beginning on 4/2/19 the total amount of the unpaid Judgment, if any, interest at the rate of 10% per annum, and shall continue to accrue until the entire Judgment and all interest is properly paid in full.

Judgment signed in open court.
